title: Behavioral conformance vectors + in-browser exerciser — how an implementer proves a component conforms when only the rendered result counts
summary: Prep research for decision #899: how an implementer proves a component conforms to a WE standard when the contract is build-agnostic (only the final rendered component is judged, never how it's built). The static manifest check (capability-manifest/check.ts, the #267 build-time check:validation-adherence) validates declared-vs-used features but CANNOT capture behavioral/temporal conformance — e.g. that validator-resolution's `versioning` default drops a stale async generation (validator-resolution/provider.ts:84-88: shouldApplyResult returns handle.version === current). The leaning model is a conformance KIT: a declarative JSON vector corpus (given setup + a possibly-timed interaction script + the observable outcome in contract vocab, read via DOM/ARIA, never internals) + vector schema + dependency-free reference verifier (assertion semantics) + a binding interface; implementers supply a binding adapter + their own browser driver and verify in their own CI with no hosted dependency (the escapable floor, minimize-lock-in); plateau hosts the zero-setup in-browser exerciser (controllable clock, dashboards, pass/fail over time) as the convenience/paid product — exactly the WPT split where tests+testharness.js are the kit anyone runs and wpt.fyi is the product. SURVEYED: (1) WPT — three artifacts, two narrow contracts (corpus ⊥ runner ⊥ dashboard); the corpus/runner seam IS the monetization line (wpt.fyi owns zero tests, runs none); testdriver.js routes in-page actions through a swappable vendor bridge; load-bearing — WPT has NO virtual clock in the corpus, time machinery lives entirely in the runner tier; detachable reporting hook (wptreport.json). (2) JSON Schema Test Suite — THE closest analog: 100% JSON corpus with NO shared runner code, each impl writes a ~30-line adapter loop, outcome kept minimal (`valid` boolean), versioned per draft, one file per keyword, an optional/ tier for non-required capabilities. (3) test262 — the $262 host object is the single named binding interface every engine fills in; async tests signal via $DONE(), the host owns the event loop. (4) ARIA-AT — invariant assertions authored once with MUST/SHOULD/MAY priority, per-impl commands vary (commands.json); aria-at-app is the hosted iframe runner collecting interop results (open-corpus + paid-hosted again). (5) Testing Library / black-box — assert on the ARIA/DOM/event surface, never impl internals; the accessibility tree is the only layer every implementation must agree on by definition. (6) @sinonjs/fake-timers + Playwright page.clock — the clean seam: a clock VERB contract (now/tick/tickAsync/next/runAll) is standardizable, the install mechanism is a swappable backend (embedded Sinon for jsdom; page.clock over CDP for real render); Vitest/Jest fake timers are thin wrappers over Sinon. IN-REPO PRIOR ART (not greenfield): WE already ships a conformance-vector + reference-target + golden-output pattern at blocks/renderers/module-service/conformance/ (vectors.ts VectorInput, golden.json, runner.ts, referenceTarget.ts, dotnetTarget.ts) — #899 generalizes that request→golden shape to behavioral/temporal vectors with DOM/ARIA observation and a controllable clock. SYNTHESIS handed to #899: run through the constellation rulings the two stated forks collapse to two forced invariants + one genuine call. Forced invariant A (#817 file-seam test): WE owns corpus + schema + assertion-semantics verifier (it IS a WE conformance gate like check.ts) + the binding interface incl. the clock verb contract; the binding+clock IMPLEMENTATION is runtime → FUI; the verifier consumes time as data (a timestamped trace), never a live clock, so no WE gate consumes the clock impl. Forced invariant B (#091 decompose / no home decision): the hosted dashboards-over-time exerciser is a served product → plateau (wpt.fyi/aria-at-app shape), a thin stateless local exerciser → FUI devtool; FUI hosting a stateful results SaaS is the broken branch. THE ONE GENUINE FORK (Fork 2 narrowed): after #817 settles the verifier as pure assertion semantics, does the WE PROJECT additionally publish a runnable reference BACKEND (clock + DOM dispatch) as zero-lock-in proof-of-runnability, or stay pure-contract and let FUI/implementers own all runnable backends? Recommended default: pure-contract — reference backend lives in FUI (open/free per open-core, floor stays escapable), keeping WE = contracts/conformance only (#817, #855, impl-is-not-a-standard); ~70-75%, residual is the WPT precedent (testharness.js ships with the corpus) + the JSON Schema Test Suite Node-binding precedent for a separate non-scoped reference package. Full grounding in reports/2026-06-18-behavioral-conformance-vectors-kit.md.
